voice_anchor: B2B institutional, Singapore-government-adjacent register (BCA / WSG / PDPC). No emojis. No exclamation marks in body copy. No marketing-speak. Operational concreteness over brand poetry. Numbers cited only where credible and non-confidential.

global.site_tagline: Singapore's FMCG in-store merchandising bench, run on EMR-Intel.
global.site_description: 350+ FMCG merchandisers across Singapore supermarkets, pharmacies, and modern trade — run on EMR-Intel, our field-force platform. A unit of Elitez Group.
global.company_legal_name: Elitez Merchandising — a business unit of Elitez Group of Companies

home.hero_headline: Singapore's FMCG in-store bench. Run on EMR-Intel.
home.hero_subhead: We deploy and manage 350+ FMCG merchandisers across supermarkets, pharmacies, and modern trade — backed by our proprietary field-force platform.
home.hero_supporting_note: A business unit of Elitez Group of Companies — WSG Human Capital Partner, IHRP exclusive recruitment partner, SkillsFuture-approved training organization.

hero.heading: Singapore's FMCG in-store bench. Run on EMR-Intel.
hero.subhead: We deploy and manage 350+ FMCG merchandisers across supermarkets, pharmacies, and modern trade — backed by our proprietary field-force platform.
hero.body: Planogram drift, share-of-shelf erosion, and out-of-stock at 4pm Friday are operational problems. We treat them as such — with a trained bench, structured routing, supervisor coverage, and live KPI dashboards visible to your brand HQ.
hero.trust_line: Trusted by L'Oréal, Unilever, Shiseido, Pernod Ricard, Reckitt, FairPrice, Oatside, Suntory.

scale-stats.heading: Singapore's largest FMCG merchandising bench.
scale-stats.body: Operational scale matters when you are running daily call cycles across hundreds of outlets. We have built the bench, the supervision layer, and the technology to keep it productive.

stats.label: FMCG manpower island-wide
stats.note: Dedicated and shared, supermarkets and pharmacies.

stats.label: Field-force retention
stats.note: Industry-leading against a category where attrition is the silent killer.

stats.label: Elitez Group operating history
stats.note: Founded 2010. SEA HR and outsourcing scale behind every contract.

stats.label: Offices across SEA
stats.note: Group footprint. Merchandising delivery is Singapore island-wide.

scale-stats.footnote: Group-level figures cited as institutional context. Merchandising service delivery is Singapore only.

service-lines.heading: Five service lines under one roof.
service-lines.body: Most agencies do dedicated, or shared, or audit. We run all five — dedicated teams, shared bench, trade-marketing execution, audit, and the EMR-Intel platform that ties them together.

items.label: Dedicated
items.title: Dedicated Merchandising
items.body: Full-time team scoped to one brand. Managed teams of 5 to 60 pax depending on coverage. Routing, KPIs, supervision, training, and free replacement on under-performance.

items.label: Shared
items.title: Shared Merchandising
items.body: Up to six pax shared across clients. Ad-hoc cover for crucial outlets across supermarkets and pharmacies. Available almost immediately, cost-efficient for brands without a full-time roster.

items.label: Trade-Marketing
items.title: Trade-Marketing Execution
items.body: POSM deployment, space-buy execution, secondary display setup, NPI launch coverage, and the field training that makes promotions land in the aisle, not just in the brief.

items.label: Audit
items.title: Merchandising Audit
items.body: Third-party in-store compliance audits. Structured channel coverage, clear lead-time reporting, and workflow tracking modelled on the 2024/25 L'Oréal merchandising and audit programme.

items.label: EMR-Intel
items.title: EMR-Intel Platform
items.body: Proprietary field-force management system bundled with every merchandising contract. Mobile app for the field, QuickView for supervisors, BI dashboards for brand HQ.

emr-intel-teaser.heading: Your field force, instrumented.
emr-intel-teaser.body:
EMR-Intel is the platform that makes the bench legible to your brand team. Every visit is GPS-stamped, every planogram check is photographed, every out-of-stock event is logged in real time. Your dashboard shows MSL compliance, share-of-shelf, share-of-display, promo compliance, and daily OOS trend without anyone exporting a spreadsheet.

The mobile app ships in English, Chinese, and Bahasa Melayu — non-trivial for a Singapore-Malaysia field force.

feature_strip.label: Mobile app
feature_strip.value: EN / CN / BM, GPS check-in, photo upload

feature_strip.label: QuickView
feature_strip.value: Supervisor back-office for routing and exceptions

feature_strip.label: BI dashboards
feature_strip.value: MSL, share-of-shelf, OOS, promo compliance — live to brand HQ

client-roster.heading: Trusted by the brands that own the aisle.
client-roster.body: A working roster, not a hall of fame. We list the brands we currently serve. Specific engagement scopes and team sizes are kept confidential.
client-roster.logos_note: L'Oréal (CPD, LUXE, PPD, ACD), Unilever, Shiseido, Pernod Ricard, Reckitt, Coty, FairPrice (house brand), Oatside, Suntory, Walch, Gardenia, Phoon Huat, F.East, Fresenius Kabi, Blood.

industries.heading: Categories we cover.
industries.body: Five FMCG categories. Each has its own merchandising rhythm — cosmetics needs counter discipline, F&B needs replenishment speed, pharma needs compliance documentation. We staff and train against the category.

items.title: Cosmetics & Beauty
items.body: L'Oréal (CPD, LUXE, PPD, ACD), Shiseido, Coty. Counter, planogram, NPI launches across Sasa, Watsons, Guardian, and department-store channels.

items.title: Personal Care & Household
items.body: Unilever, Reckitt, Walch. Supermarket aisle coverage, secondary display, promotion run-out, replenishment monitoring.

items.title: Food & Beverage
items.body: Pernod Ricard, Oatside, Suntory, Gardenia, Phoon Huat, F.East. Replenishment, share-of-shelf defence, end-cap execution.

items.title: Pharma & Health
items.body: Fresenius Kabi, Blood. Pharmacy-channel merchandising with documentation and compliance posture appropriate to the category.

items.title: Retail House-Brand
items.body: FairPrice. Embedded merchandising for retail-owned private-label SKUs across the chain.

channels.heading: Every channel that matters in Singapore.
channels.body: Supermarkets, pharmacies, convenience, and specialty. Coverage tuned to where your category sells, with route plans built around outlet density and turn rate.

items.label: Supermarkets
items.body: FairPrice, Cold Storage, Giant, Sheng Siong.

items.label: Pharmacies
items.body: Watsons, Guardian, Unity.

items.label: Convenience
items.body: 7-Eleven, Cheers.

items.label: Specialty & Department
items.body: Sasa, Decathlon, beauty counters.

accreditations.heading: Credentials your procurement team will check.
accreditations.body: Government-aligned and audited. The certifications below are held at the Elitez Group level and apply to merchandising service delivery.

differentiators.heading: Why brand teams switch to us.
differentiators.body: Four reasons buyers cite in renewal conversations. None of them are about price.

items.title: EMR-Intel, not Excel and WhatsApp
items.body: Most Singapore merch agencies still run on spreadsheets and group chats. We instrument the field force with a platform your brand HQ can read live.

items.title: Dedicated and shared, one roof
items.body: Most agencies do one or the other. Buyers move between models as their roster evolves — we can hold both without re-onboarding.

items.title: Retention is the moat
items.body: Above 95% field retention. Attrition is the silent killer of merchandising programmes; we operate the bench like a permanent business, not a contractor pool.

items.title: Free replacement on under-performance
items.body: If a merchandiser is not pulling weight, we swap them at no extra cost. The penalty for under-performance sits with us, not with you.

cta.heading: Speak to Jack Wang.
cta.body: Fifteen minutes is enough to scope dedicated versus shared, walk through EMR-Intel, and check whether we are the right bench for your brand.

services.hero_headline: Five service lines. One operational bench.
services.hero_subhead: Dedicated teams, shared cover, trade-marketing execution, audit, and the EMR-Intel platform — scoped to your roster and your KPIs.

services-intro.heading: Five service lines. One operational bench.
services-intro.body: Dedicated teams, shared cover, trade-marketing execution, audit, and the EMR-Intel platform — scoped to your roster and your KPIs. Engagement is structured around your in-store execution metrics, not a generic service catalogue.

service-dedicated.heading: A full-time team scoped to one brand.
service-dedicated.body:
Your bench, badged, trained, and supervised. Team sizes range from 5 to 60 pax depending on coverage. We handle recruitment, training, routing, supervision, KPI reporting, and the replacement of any under-performer at no additional cost.

Recommended when in-store execution is a sustained, year-round KPI — e.g., share-of-shelf defence, planogram compliance, promotional run-outs, NPI coverage, and competitor monitoring across hundreds of outlets per week.
features: Recruitment, onboarding, and structured training
Daily routing and outlet coverage planning
Supervisor layer with field audits and exception handling
KPI reporting against MSL, share-of-shelf, OOS, planogram, promo compliance
Free replacement of any merchandiser on under-performance
EMR-Intel bundled — mobile app, QuickView, BI dashboards
service-dedicated.best_for: Brands with sustained in-store presence and multi-million-dollar trade investment to protect.

service-shared.heading: Auxiliary cover, without a full-time roster.
service-shared.body:
Up-to-six-pax shared bench providing ad-hoc coverage across supermarkets, pharmacies, and convenience. Available on short notice. Cost-efficient for brands whose coverage needs are seasonal, geographically narrow, or layered on top of an existing team.

Shared bench operates on the same EMR-Intel platform as the dedicated model — your dashboard does not change when you scale up or down.
features: Ad-hoc and recurring cover across selected outlets
Same supervision standard as the dedicated bench
EMR-Intel reporting across the assigned routes
Turn-key onboarding measured in days, not weeks
Scale up to a dedicated team without re-tendering
service-shared.best_for: Challenger brands, seasonal SKUs, NPI launches, and gap-filling around an existing in-house team.

service-trade-marketing.heading: Promotions land in the aisle, not just in the brief.
service-trade-marketing.body:
POSM deployment, space-buy execution, secondary display build-out, and NPI launch coverage. Trade-marketing execution sits between your campaign team and the retailer — we close the gap that is usually filled by goodwill and weekend favours.

Retailers move quickly; your trade calendar does not always sync with theirs. We hold the field-level relationships and the staging capacity to convert a brief into a built display by the agreed launch date.
features: POSM staging, transport, and in-aisle installation
Secondary display and end-cap build-out
Space-buy execution across major retail chains
NPI launch coverage with photo evidence and compliance reporting
Field training for promoter-driven activations
service-trade-marketing.best_for: Brand teams running NPI launches, seasonal campaigns, or end-cap takeovers in tier-1 retail.

service-audit.heading: Third-party verification of what is actually in-store.
service-audit.body:
When you need an independent read of in-store execution — yours or someone else's — we field a structured audit programme with clear channel coverage, lead-time reporting, and workflow tracking modelled on the 2024/25 L'Oréal merchandising and audit programme.

Audits are run by trained auditors, not the same teams executing day-to-day merchandising, so the read is independent and defensible.
features: Structured outlet sampling across supermarkets and pharmacies
Planogram, share-of-shelf, OOS, pricing, and promo compliance checks
Workflow tracking with clear lead-time reporting
Photo evidence and exception logs in EMR-Intel
Independent auditor pool — segregated from execution teams
service-audit.best_for: Brand HQ teams verifying field execution, or retailers auditing supplier compliance.

service-emr-intel-link.heading: Every service line runs on EMR-Intel.
service-emr-intel-link.body: The platform is not sold separately. It is the operating system underneath every contract — mobile app for the field force, QuickView for supervisors, BI dashboards for your brand HQ. Multilingual EN, CN, BM.

engagement-model.heading: How a contract starts.
engagement-model.body: A predictable on-boarding sequence. Each step is owned by a named person on our side — no diffuse accountability.

steps.title: Scoping call
steps.body: Fifteen minutes with Jack Wang. We confirm category, channels, coverage scope, and KPI priorities.

steps.title: Proposal
steps.body: Written scope within one week — headcount, routing, KPI definitions, EMR-Intel configuration, and commercials.

steps.title: Recruitment & training
steps.body: We draw from the existing bench wherever possible. Net-new headcount goes through structured recruitment and category-specific training.

steps.title: Deployment & shake-out
steps.body: First four weeks are a deliberate calibration window — routes adjusted, KPIs locked, supervisor escalation paths verified.

steps.title: Steady-state
steps.body: Weekly KPI review, monthly business review, quarterly strategy review. EMR-Intel dashboards are live to your team throughout.

replacement-promise.heading: Under-performance is our cost, not yours.
replacement-promise.body: If a merchandiser is not pulling weight against agreed KPIs, we replace them at no extra cost. The penalty for under-performance sits with the agency, not with the brand. It is the simplest version of an accountability promise we have been able to write.

cta.heading: Scope it with Jack Wang.
cta.body: Bring your category, your channel list, and your KPI definitions. Fifteen minutes is enough to confirm fit.

emr-intel.hero_subhead: Mobile app, supervisor back-office, BI dashboards. Bundled with every contract. Multilingual EN, CN, BM. Live to your brand HQ.

hero.heading: Your field force, instrumented.
hero.subhead: Mobile app, supervisor back-office, BI dashboards. Bundled with every contract. Multilingual EN, CN, BM. Live to your brand HQ.
hero.body: EMR-Intel is not a product we sell. It is the operating system underneath every Elitez merchandising contract. Your team gets the mobile app; your supervisors get the back-office; your brand HQ gets the dashboards.
hero.trust_line: Used live across every Elitez merchandising deployment in Singapore.

platform-pillars.heading: Three pillars. One platform.
platform-pillars.body: The same data, surfaced three ways. Field workers see their next visit. Supervisors see exceptions. Brand HQ sees the KPI roll-up. Nothing is exported, re-keyed, or screenshotted.

items.title: Mobile app for the field
items.body: GPS check-in, journey planning, planogram capture, stock and price check, share-of-shelf, photo upload, eContent SOP reference. EN / CN / BM.

items.title: QuickView back-office
items.body: Supervisor dashboard for routing, attendance, exception handling, and live audit response. Built around the day-shape of a field supervisor, not a corporate analyst.

items.title: BI dashboards for brand HQ
items.body: Executive summary, weekly call achievement, MSL compliance, share-of-shelf, share-of-display, promo compliance, daily OOS trend, competitor price-check, photo gallery, attendance.

mobile-app.heading: What your merchandiser sees.
mobile-app.body:
The mobile app is the front door of every visit. Your merchandiser logs in, sees their route, walks the store, captures evidence, and closes the visit — all without a back-office handoff.

Multilingual support is non-trivial. Singapore-Malaysia field forces are mixed EN, CN, BM; the app ships natively in all three so training does not get lost in translation.
capabilities: Attendance and GPS check-in at the outlet
Planogram compliance capture with reference photos
eContent reference — SOPs, brand books, training videos
Languages: English, Mandarin Chinese, Bahasa Melayu

quickview-backoffice.heading: What your supervisor sees.
quickview-backoffice.body: Supervisors do not have time to navigate a BI tool between store visits. QuickView is built for the field-supervisor day — fast attendance review, exception triage, route adjustment, and audit response — on whatever device they have to hand.
capabilities: Live attendance roster with exception flags
Escalation path to client account manager

bi-dashboards.heading: What your brand HQ sees.
bi-dashboards.body: Your dashboard, not ours. The BI layer is configured per contract — KPI definitions, threshold colours, outlet groupings, and channel cuts are all tuned to how your trade-marketing team already reads the business.

dashboards.title: Executive Summary
dashboards.body: Single-screen read of weekly call achievement, MSL compliance, share-of-shelf, OOS, and promo compliance.

dashboards.title: Weekly Call Achievement
dashboards.body: Planned versus achieved visits, by route, by merchandiser, by channel.

dashboards.title: MSL Compliance
dashboards.body: Must-stock-list adherence across outlets, with photo-evidenced exception logging.

dashboards.title: Share-of-Shelf / Share-of-Display
dashboards.body: Linear shelf measurement against competitor SKUs, with end-cap and secondary-display roll-up.

dashboards.title: Promo Compliance
dashboards.body: Promotion run-out verification — pricing, signage, end-cap, planogram.

dashboards.title: Daily OOS Trend
dashboards.body: Out-of-stock by SKU, by outlet, by day. Trend lines flag persistent versus transient gaps.

dashboards.title: Competitor Price-Check
dashboards.body: Price tracking on adjacent SKUs, captured during the visit.

dashboards.title: Photo Gallery
dashboards.body: Every visit produces evidence. Searchable by outlet, date, SKU, and exception type.

dashboards.title: Attendance & Activity
dashboards.body: Field-force time on store, time at outlet, and route compliance.

kpi-coverage.heading: What we measure.
kpi-coverage.body: The KPIs below are the default coverage. Definitions are negotiated per contract — your trade-marketing leads tell us how each metric is computed, and we configure the platform to match.

matrix.definition: Must-stock-list adherence at outlet level, evidenced by photo capture.
Linear shelf-space measurement against direct competitors in the same fixture.
Off-shelf and secondary-display presence — end-caps, gondolas, dump bins.
Adherence to the brand planogram, captured against a reference image.
SKU-level stock gaps logged at visit time, with persistent-versus-transient trend.
Pricing, signage, and display verification against the agreed campaign brief.
Planned versus achieved visits by route and merchandiser.
Captured prices on adjacent SKUs, by category, by outlet.

languages.heading: English. Mandarin Chinese. Bahasa Melayu.
languages.body: The Singapore-Malaysia field force is mixed-language. Training translates better when the platform speaks the same language as the person walking the aisle. Every screen in the mobile app is available in all three.

integration-note.heading: How EMR-Intel meets your stack.
integration-note.body:
EMR-Intel exports to Power BI, Tableau, and most enterprise BI tools your trade-marketing team already uses. CSV and scheduled email reports are standard. For deeper integration — direct database read access or scheduled SFTP drops — talk to us during scoping; we have done both before with multinational brand HQs.

PDPA stance: Singapore-resident data, role-based access control, audit logging, and ISO 27001 alignment via the Elitez Group certification.

cta.heading: Walk through the platform.
cta.body: Thirty minutes with Jack Wang. We will share screen, walk through a live deployment, and answer integration questions.

industries.body: Each FMCG category has its own merchandising discipline. Cosmetics needs counter ownership and brand-trained promoters. F&B needs replenishment speed at the shelf. Pharma needs documentation. We staff and train against the category, not against a generic field-force template.

case-framing.heading: The shape of a typical engagement.
case-framing.body: We do not publish account stories. The patterns below describe the typical shape of an engagement — they are not specific to any one client.

patterns.title: Dedicated for a tier-1 multinational
patterns.body: Full-time team scoped to a single multi-brand portfolio. Daily call cycles across supermarkets and pharmacies. Weekly KPI review with the trade-marketing lead. EMR-Intel dashboards configured to brand-specific SKU and planogram references.

patterns.title: Shared cover for a challenger brand
patterns.body: Two-to-four pax from the shared bench covering a curated outlet list. Used as a wedge into modern trade before scaling to dedicated. EMR-Intel applied identically to the dedicated model — no platform change on scale-up.

patterns.title: Independent audit across a chain
patterns.body: Trained auditors, segregated from execution teams, running structured outlet sampling and compliance verification with clear lead-time reporting.

retention-stat.heading: Above 95% field-force retention.
retention-stat.body: Attrition is the silent killer of merchandising programmes. New faces mean retraining, slower routes, lower MSL accuracy, and broken outlet relationships. We operate the bench like a permanent business — career paths, supervisor progression, structured training — and the retention number is the output of running it that way.

cta.heading: Join the roster.
cta.body: Fifteen minutes with Jack Wang to confirm fit, scope, and timing.

about.hero_headline: A business unit of Elitez Group of Companies.
about.hero_subhead: 15 years of human-capital operations across SEA. WSG Human Capital Partner. IHRP exclusive recruitment partner. SkillsFuture-approved training organization.

story.heading: A business unit of Elitez Group of Companies.
story.body:
Elitez Merchandising is the FMCG in-store arm of Elitez Group — a Singapore-headquartered human-capital firm founded in 2010, now operating across nine offices in Southeast Asia. The merchandising business is run as a focused unit because in-store execution is its own discipline, distinct from the group's broader HR, recruitment, and outsourcing lines.

We built the merchandising bench, the supervisor layer, and EMR-Intel in deliberate sequence — bench first, supervision second, technology third — because operational reliability is what brand teams actually buy.

parent-group.heading: Backed by Elitez Group of Companies.
parent-group.body: Elitez Group is a Ministry-of-Manpower-recognised Human Capital Partner, an IHRP exclusive recruitment partner, and a SkillsFuture-approved training organization. The accreditations above belong to the group; the merchandising unit inherits all of them.

group-metrics.heading: Operational scale behind every merchandising contract.
group-metrics.body: Group-level figures shown as institutional context. Merchandising service delivery is Singapore island-wide.

stats.label: Payroll processed
stats.note: Headcount processed across Elitez Group payroll services.

stats.label: Outsourced placements
stats.note: Field, retail, and operational placements across SEA.

stats.label: Recruitment placements
stats.note: Cumulative placements across permanent and contract searches.

leadership.heading: Who runs merchandising day-to-day.
leadership.body: Jack Wang is the named contact for new and existing merchandising engagements. Executive escalation routes through the Elitez Group leadership team.

primary.name: Jack Wang
primary.title: Associate Director, Elitez Merchandising
primary.bio: Jack leads merchandising client engagement at Elitez. Operating background in FMCG field operations, with day-to-day ownership of dedicated and shared programmes across the current client roster.

accreditations.heading: Group-level certifications.
accreditations.body: Audited at the Elitez Group level and applied to merchandising service delivery.

compliance-note.heading: PDPA, ISO 27001, MOM-aligned.
compliance-note.body:
Field-collected data is treated with the same posture as a regulated employer. Singapore-resident data, role-based access control in EMR-Intel, audit logging, and an ISO 27001 information-security regime via the Elitez Group certification. PDPA stance is documented and shared on request during procurement.

Workforce compliance — MOM work-pass administration, employment contracts, statutory contributions — sits inside the group's Human Capital Partnership operating model.

office-locations.heading: Singapore HQ. Regional footprint.
office-locations.body: Merchandising service delivery is Singapore only. The group's regional offices are listed below as institutional context for procurement and audit teams.

primary.label: Singapore HQ
primary.note: Merchandising operations, leadership, and client engagement.

office-locations.regional_note: Elitez Group operates additional offices across Malaysia, Indonesia, Vietnam, and the Philippines for recruitment and outsourcing service lines. These are not merchandising-delivery offices.

owner-card.body: Jack handles new merchandising engagements end-to-end — scoping call, written proposal, deployment, and steady-state account review.

contact-form.heading: Send a brief.
contact-form.body: We acknowledge every submission within one business day. Use the form for written briefs; use the phone or email above if you need to speak today.
contact-form.success_message: Brief received. Jack will respond within one business day.

office-address.map_caption: By appointment only — please confirm a time with Jack before visiting.

faq.q: What is the difference between dedicated and shared merchandising?
faq.a:
**Dedicated** is a full-time team scoped to one brand — managed headcount ranges from 5 to 60 pax depending on coverage. Routing, KPIs, supervision, and training are all yours.

**Shared** is an up-to-six-pax bench providing ad-hoc coverage across multiple clients. Faster to deploy, more cost-efficient, and ideal for seasonal SKUs, NPI launches, or gap-filling around an existing team.

Both run on EMR-Intel, and you can move between models without re-tendering.

Engagement-based, scoped to headcount, channels covered, and EMR-Intel configuration. We do not publish list prices because every brief has a different shape. After a fifteen-minute scoping call, we issue a written proposal within one week.

Shared bench cover can be live within days where existing trained merchandisers fit the brief. Dedicated programmes typically run a four-to-six-week mobilisation window — recruitment where required, category-specific training, route building, and a deliberate four-week calibration phase before steady-state.

faq.q: Field-force attrition kills programmes. How do you beat it?
faq.a: We run the bench like a permanent business — structured training, supervisor progression, internal mobility, and a career path that does not assume merchandising is a transient role. The current retention rate is above 95 percent annually, well ahead of category norms.

faq.q: What happens if a merchandiser is underperforming?
faq.a: We replace them at no additional cost. The penalty for under-performance sits with us, not with you. Underperformance is defined against the KPIs agreed at scoping — MSL compliance, call achievement, share-of-shelf, and the brand-specific metrics in your proposal.

faq.q: How does EMR-Intel integrate with our brand HQ systems?
faq.a: EMR-Intel exports to Power BI, Tableau, and most enterprise BI tools — CSV and scheduled email reports are standard. For deeper integration (direct database read or scheduled SFTP drops to your data team) we scope that during proposal. We have done this with several multinational brand HQs.

faq.q: Does the mobile app support multiple languages?
faq.a: Yes — English, Mandarin Chinese, and Bahasa Melayu, natively. This matters because the Singapore-Malaysia field-force is mixed-language and training translates better when the platform speaks the same language as the person walking the aisle.

PDPA-aligned. Singapore-resident data. Role-based access control in EMR-Intel. Audit logging on all field-collected records. ISO 27001 information-security regime via the Elitez Group certification, plus Cyber Essentials Mark. PDPA documentation is shared on request during procurement.

faq.q: Do you cover Malaysia, Indonesia, or other SEA markets?
faq.a:
**In-store merchandising is Singapore-only.** That is the focused service line of Elitez Merchandising. 

Elitez Group operates across nine SEA offices for recruitment, outsourcing, and HR service lines — but the merchandising bench, supervisor layer, and EMR-Intel deployment are Singapore-island-wide. If you need merchandising coverage outside Singapore, we can refer you, but we will not staff it ourselves.

faq.q: Can you audit our existing agency's work?
faq.a: Yes. The audit service line is run by trained auditors segregated from execution teams, so the read is independent and defensible. Structured outlet sampling, photo evidence, planogram and OOS verification, and lead-time-clear reporting. Modelled on the 2024/25 L'Oréal merchandising and audit programme.

faq.q: What categories do you have most depth in?
faq.a: Cosmetics and beauty (the L'Oréal portfolio across CPD, LUXE, PPD, and ACD, plus Shiseido and Coty); personal care and household (Unilever, Reckitt, Walch); F&B (Pernod Ricard, Oatside, Suntory, Gardenia, Phoon Huat, F.East); pharma (Fresenius Kabi, Blood); and retail house-brand (FairPrice). Roughly five categories, each with category-trained merchandisers and supervisors.

faq.q: What is the relationship to Elitez Group of Companies?
faq.a: Elitez Merchandising is a business unit of Elitez Group of Companies. The group is a WSG Human Capital Partner, IHRP exclusive recruitment partner, and SkillsFuture-approved training organization. Accreditations, ISO certifications, and compliance regime all sit at the group level and apply to merchandising service delivery.

notes_for_human: Pricing is intentionally not published per brief.constraints — FAQ answers with 'engagement-based, scoped to headcount + tech stack'.

Client headcounts (e.g. 'L'Oréal 14 pax', 'Unilever 60 pax') from the pitch deck are NOT used per brief.constraints. Copy uses abstract ranges only ('350+ FMCG manpower island-wide', 'managed teams of 5-60 pax per client').

Group-level metrics (311,960 payroll processed, 263,138 outsourced placements, 6,228 placements) are sourced from brief.json narrative_brief.value_props[5]. If these need updating, update brief.json first and regenerate copy.json.
Retention figure '>95%' is sourced from brief.json. If procurement teams ask for a methodology note, we should commission a one-page methodology brief from the operations team.

Group-level certifications listed (Human Capital Partnership, Cyber Essentials, ISO 9001:2015, ISO 27001, Bizsafe 3, CPLC, EcoVadis Bronze) are from brief.narrative_brief.accreditations_relevant. Confirm with operations that each is currently active before public launch.

Client roster on /clients.html lists brand families that are L'Oréal-public knowledge (CPD/LUXE/PPD/ACD portfolio brands). Some smaller-brand entries (e.g. 'Blood', 'F.East') may benefit from a one-line description on first publication — flagged with generic category placeholders for now.

case-framing section uses GENERIC engagement patterns (no client-specific stories) per brief.constraints. If a client gives written permission for a named case study post-launch, we can swap the generic pattern for a real story.

EMR-Intel 'Power BI / Tableau / SFTP' integration capabilities are sourced from brief.narrative_brief.service_lines[2]. If the actual platform team confirms a narrower or wider integration surface, update copy.json.pages.emr-intel.sections.integration-note accordingly.
Jack Wang's bio paragraph in leadership section is GENERIC — flagged for the operations team to write a 1-2 sentence personalised bio with real experience details before public launch.

Address '2 Kallang Avenue #03-08, CT Hub, Singapore 339407' is from brief.narrative_brief.company_metadata. Confirm before public launch this remains the correct primary office for merchandising client visits.

FAQ on /contact.html is keyed by section id 'faq' and the actual FAQ entries live in copy.json.faq[] (top-level) per FIELD-DICTIONARY pattern. Agent 4 should render copy.json.faq[] under the contact page faq section.

Footer column groupings (services / company / platform / contact) are a recommendation — Agent 4 may consolidate to a simpler footer if the visual design calls for it. All link labels are in footer.links.
All sitemap section ids are covered. colors.html is correctly skipped (internal:true).
Deck PDF '/elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf' is referenced as a download target but the PDF asset itself is not produced by Agent 5 — flag for Agent 6 or a follow-up to produce/upload the actual PDF.
